default_scene.note: Per AIR row, ProofShapeAir observes preprocessed_commit or log_height on TranscriptBus and range-checks log_height to [0, 32) via PowerCheckerBus, which also ties height = 2^log_height.

power-lookup.note: PS1a: log_height is looked up on PowerCheckerBus, which simultaneously range-checks it to [0, 32) and enforces the relation height = 2^log_height.

commit-registration.note: When an AIR has preprocessed data, the preprocessed commit is absorbed on TranscriptBus and simultaneously provided on CommitmentsBus so MerkleVerifyAir can authenticate it during WHIR.

preamble-4.constraints_html: ProofShapeAir observes preprocessed_commit (DIGEST_SIZE elements, VK-fixed) or log_height depending on the AIR's VK metadata. Preprocessed commitments are additionally registered on CommitmentsBus. PS1a range-checks log_height to [0, 32) via PowerCheckerBus, which simultaneously enforces height = 2^log_height.
preamble-4.soundness_html: PowerCheckerBus lookup guarantees log_height in range and ties height to 2^log_height by table correctness (LT). The preprocessed commit is VK-fixed per AIR, so its value is determined by the child VK; CommitmentsBus routes it to MerkleVerifyAir for authentication when the corresponding AIR's cached trace is opened.

default_scene.note: Each cached commitment per AIR is observed on TranscriptBus and registered on CommitmentsBus so MerkleVerifyAir can authenticate it; in continuations, CachedCommitBus also routes it to the enclosing circuit.

transcript-observe.note: Each cached_commits[i] (DIGEST_SIZE elements) is absorbed into the transcript; the VK-fixed count of cached commitments per AIR determines how many transcript slots are consumed.

cached-continuations.note: When continuations are enabled, the same cached commitment is also sent on CachedCommitBus so the enclosing continuations circuit can verify it against the child VK.

same-cell.note: The critical invariant: the same trace cell feeds both the TranscriptBus observe and the CommitmentsBus provide, pinning the cached commitment to both the transcript and later Merkle verification.

preamble-5.constraints_html: ProofShapeAir observes each cached_commits[i] (DIGEST_SIZE elements) on TranscriptBus and registers it on CommitmentsBus for later Merkle root authentication during WHIR. CachedCommitBus is used for circuit-internal continuations support.
preamble-5.soundness_html: The number of cached commitments per AIR is VK-fixed, so the trace row structure enforces the correct count (verify_proof_shape line 343). The same cell drives the transcript observation and the CommitmentsBus send, pinning the cached commitment value to both the transcript and Merkle verification.

default_scene.note: PublicValuesAir iterates one row per public value, receives (tidx, value) from TranscriptBus and sends (air_idx, pv_idx, value) on PublicValuesBus to the constraint evaluator. ProofShapeAir pre-announces the per-AIR PV count on NumPublicValuesBus.

count-handoff.note: ProofShapeAir announces (air_idx, tidx, num_pvs) per AIR on NumPublicValuesBus; PublicValuesAir consumes this to know where each AIR's PV block starts in the transcript.

flow.note: Each PV row is a bridge: TranscriptBus receive (tidx, value, is_sample=0) on the left, PublicValuesBus send (air_idx, pv_idx, value) on the right. The same value cell serves both.

transition.note: PV rows advance pv_idx within an AIR and air_idx across AIRs; is_first_air resets pv_idx to 0.

preamble-6.constraints_html: ProofShapeAir sends (air_idx, tidx, num_pvs) on NumPublicValuesBus where num_pvs is a VK constant. PublicValuesAir has one row per public value, receives from NumPublicValuesBus, and for each row: (a) receives (tidx, value, is_sample=0) from TranscriptBus, (b) sends (air_idx, pv_idx, value) on PublicValuesBus to SymbolicExpressionAir.
preamble-6.soundness_html: Because the same value cell drives both the TranscriptBus receive and the PublicValuesBus send, the extracted public values are exactly the transcript-observed values AND exactly the values used in constraint evaluation. This matches the reference verifier, which observes PVs and then passes the same vector to verify_zerocheck_and_logup.

default_scene.note: Non-increasing heights are enforced by range-checking log_height[i] - log_height[i+1] on RangeCheckerBus, and log_height is additionally bounded via the PowerCheckerBus lookup.

transition.note: Non-increasing heights: adjacent rows must satisfy log_height[i] ≥ log_height[i+1]. The difference is range-checked on RangeCheckerBus.

range-lookup.note: ProofShapeAir looks up log_height on PowerCheckerBus (which transitively range-checks via RangeCheckerBus). The 256-row RangeCheckerAir table is the sole provider of RangeCheckerBus entries.

shape-publish.note: Shape validation also provides the AirShapeBus and LiftedHeightsBus tables so downstream modules can look up per-AIR structural constants.

preamble-7.constraints_html: ProofShapeAir range-checks log_height (via PowerCheckerBus, which enforces height = 2^log_height) and enforces non-increasing heights (log_height[i] - log_height[i+1] ≥ 0 via RangeCheckerBus). The idx_encoder validates log_height bounds (log_height ≤ l_skip + n_stack). The number of cached-commitment columns per AIR is VK-fixed in the trace.
preamble-7.soundness_html: All shape validations distribute across (i) VK-fixed trace structure (num_airs, max_cached, num_pvs are circuit constants so the trace row count is correct by construction), (ii) range checks via RangeCheckerBus/PowerCheckerBus (both Primitive Lookup Tables enforce correct entries — LT), and (iii) bus balancing (e.g., stacking OpeningClaimsAir range-checks l_skip + n_stack - log_lifted_height via EqBitsLookupBus, so negative values fail). See proof-shape-validation.md.

default_scene.note: The summary row (is_last = 1) accumulates total weighted interaction count across the proof, decomposes it into 8-bit limbs, and range-checks each limb on RangeCheckerBus. The bound total_interactions < max_interaction_count is baked in at circuit construction.

boundary.note: PS1c: the last valid row of each proof (is_last = 1) is the summary row. It carries the accumulated total interaction count; the bound total_interactions < max_interaction_count is circuit-constant (LT).

transition.note: Adjacent rows accumulate: n_logup[i+1] = n_logup[i] + num_interactions[i] * lifted_height[i]. The accumulator lives in n_logup and is finalized on the summary row.

limb-decomp.note: On the summary row, total_interactions is split into 8-bit limbs and each limb is looked up on RangeCheckerBus. Table correctness (LT) bounds the accumulator.

preamble-8.constraints_html: ProofShapeAir's summary row (last valid row per proof) accumulates total_interactions = sum_i(num_interactions[i] * lifted_height[i]) incrementally, decomposes the result into limbs, and range-checks each limb via RangeCheckerBus. The constraint total_interactions < max_interaction_count is baked into the circuit from the child VK (PS1c).
preamble-8.soundness_html: The single max_interaction_count bound is verified at circuit construction time (VerifierSubCircuit::new) to imply every TraceHeightConstraint in the child VK: for each VK constraint, coefficient[i] ≤ num_interactions[i] and threshold ≥ max_interaction_count. Therefore any witness satisfying PS1c's summary-row bound also satisfies all child VK constraints. The limb range checks are sound by RangeCheckerAir's table correctness (LT).

default_scene.note: ProofShapeAir has one row per child AIR, indexed by sorted_idx. The sort enforces non-increasing log_height; absent AIRs (is_present = 0) are forced to the end with log_height = 0. The (sorted_idx, air_id) mapping is a bijection.

transition.note: Adjacent sorted rows enforce non-increasing log_height. Tiebreakers within a height tie are a prover choice (sound because all downstream data is keyed by the same sorted_idx).

absent-last.note: Absent AIRs have is_present = 0 and log_height = 0. They are pinned to the end of the sort by the non-increasing constraint.

bijection.note: An internal permutation bus within ProofShapeAir (ProofShapePermutationBus) forces the sorted_idx -> air_id map to be a bijection.

preamble-9.constraints_html: ProofShapeAir has one row per AIR indexed by sorted_idx (0..num_airs-1). It constrains non-increasing heights via RangeCheckerBus, forces absent AIRs last (log_height = 0 when is_present = 0), and enforces the sorted_idx -> air_id mapping is a bijection via an internal permutation bus.
preamble-9.soundness_html: The tiebreaker within a height tie is a prover choice, which is sound because: (i) all downstream data is keyed by the same sorted_idx, so reordering within a tie relabels everything consistently, and (ii) the reference verifier's algorithm only depends on non-increasing heights, not on which specific AIR occupies each sort position within a tie.

default_scene.note: On the summary row, ProofShapeAir emits all per-proof module handoff messages at once: GkrModuleBus kicks off GKR, FractionFolderInputBus starts BatchConstraint, and EqNsNLogupMaxBus/ExpressionClaimNMaxBus pre-populate the eq-polynomial lookup tables.

per-air-metadata.note: Per-AIR rows publish (sort_idx, num_interactions, need_rot) on AirShapeBus, (sort_idx, n_abs, n_sign_bit) on HyperdimBus, (air_idx, n_lift) on NLiftBus, and (sort_idx, n_lift, n_logup, num_interactions) on Eq3bShapeBus.

per-proof-handoff.note: The summary row emits per-proof constants: tidx_end and GKR dimensions on GkrModuleBus, (n_logup, n_max) on EqNsNLogupMaxBus, n_max alone on ExpressionClaimNMaxBus, num_present_airs on FractionFolderInputBus.

eq3b-shape.note: Eq3bShapeBus carries per-AIR (sort_idx, n_lift, n_logup, num_interactions) as a lookup table that Eq3bAir consults when computing the partial eq polynomial.

preamble-10.constraints_html: ProofShapeAir sends per-AIR metadata: (i, air_idx, num_interactions, need_rot) on AirShapeBus; (i, n_abs, n_sign_bit) on HyperdimBus; (air_idx, n_lift) on NLiftBus; (i, n_lift, n_logup, num_interactions) on Eq3bShapeBus. Per-proof: (tidx_end, n_logup, n_max, is_n_max_greater) on GkrModuleBus; (n_logup, n_max) on EqNsNLogupMaxBus; (n_max) on ExpressionClaimNMaxBus; (num_present_airs) on FractionFolderInputBus.
preamble-10.soundness_html: All derived quantities are deterministic functions of the extracted attributes and VK constants (PS1c). The bus messages are the only way downstream modules access these quantities; since TranscriptBus/AirShapeBus/etc. are permutation buses, any attempt to send inconsistent values would cause imbalance with the receiving AIRs' constrained loop boundaries (see M-GKR, M-BC, M-Stack, M-WHIR extraction arguments).

default_scene.note: GkrInputAir observes the logup PoW witness on TranscriptBus and samples the PoW challenge; it then looks up on ExpBitsLenBus to verify that the sample has the required low-zero-bits difficulty.

lookup.note: ExpBitsLenAir provides the global ExpBitsLenBus table; GkrInputAir's lookup succeeds iff pow_sample has pow_bits low zero bits.

observe-sample.note: The Fiat-Shamir pattern: observe pow_witness, then sample pow_sample. GkrInputAir receives both messages on TranscriptBus at consecutive tidx.

gkr-1.constraints_html: GkrInputAir observes logup_pow_witness on TranscriptBus and samples logup_pow_sample. It then issues an ExpBitsLenBus lookup that verifies the sampled value has pow_bits low zero bits.
gkr-1.soundness_html: ExpBitsLenAir's table correctness (LT) guarantees that the lookup succeeds iff the PoW condition holds. The observation/sample pair is tied to the Poseidon2 sponge (T), so the sample is a deterministic function of the observation, and the prover must do pow_bits work to find a favorable witness.

default_scene.note: GkrInputAir samples alpha_logup and sends the transcript index for beta on InteractionsFoldingInputBus; BatchConstraint's InteractionsFoldingAir samples beta at that reserved slot inside GKR's transcript range.

handoff.note: InteractionsFoldingInputBus is the permutation handoff: GkrInputAir sends tidx_if, InteractionsFoldingAir receives it and places its beta sample at that transcript position.

interleaved-samples.note: alpha (GKR) and beta (BC) are sampled at consecutive transcript slots. Per-tidx ownership of TranscriptBus ensures exactly one consumer per tidx even across modules.

gkr-2.constraints_html: GkrInputAir samples alpha_logup at tidx = tidx_end + pow_offset. It reserves a D_EF-wide gap for beta_logup at tidx_if = tidx_end + pow_offset + D_EF and sends tidx_if on InteractionsFoldingInputBus so BatchConstraint's InteractionsFoldingAir can sample beta_logup at the reserved position.
gkr-2.soundness_html: alpha_logup and beta_logup are transcript samples pinned by the Poseidon2 sponge (T). Although BC samples beta within GKR's transcript range, the TranscriptBus permutation check still holds because each tidx is owned by exactly one AIR (GKR owns alpha, BC owns beta at tidx_if). The handoff tidx_if is a deterministic function of tidx_end and pow_bits.

default_scene.note: GkrInputAir observes q0_claim (DIGEST_SIZE = D_EF elements) on TranscriptBus. When n_logup = 0 the root denominator claim is fixed to 1 by construction and no transcript slot is consumed.

observe.note: The observed q0_claim is the only non-zero input to GKR's root layer; it pins the LogUp root denominator sum on the transcript.

boundary.note: Degenerate case n_logup = 0: q0_claim is not observed (no LogUp argument), so the row's transcript-receive multiplicity is zero. The GKR chain skips directly to xi sampling.

gkr-3.constraints_html: GkrInputAir observes q0_claim (D_EF) on TranscriptBus. When n_logup = 0, q0_claim is set to 1 by construction and no transcript slot is used.
gkr-3.soundness_html: The root claim is pinned on the transcript. GkrLayerAir's root-layer boundary constraint then enforces q_cross == q0_claim (verified at the root layer), so any GKR reduction must match the observed root claim.

default_scene.note: Root layer (layer_idx = 0): observe p_xi_0, q_xi_0, p_xi_1, q_xi_1 on TranscriptBus, sample mu, and enforce p_cross = 0, q_cross = q0_claim. The sampled mu becomes the first xi coordinate.

boundary.note: Root-layer boundary: is_first = 1 enforces p_cross = 0 and q_cross = q0_claim (received via GkrLayerInputBus from GkrInputAir).

observe-and-sample.note: Four observations (p_xi_0, q_xi_0, p_xi_1, q_xi_1) followed by one mu sample. Linear interpolation with mu produces the next layer's claim.

gkr-4.constraints_html: GkrLayerAir observes (p_xi_0, q_xi_0, p_xi_1, q_xi_1) on TranscriptBus and samples mu. On the root layer (layer_idx = 0), it enforces p_cross = 0 and q_cross = q0_claim (zero-check / root consistency). It then computes the next layer's claim via linear interpolation: claim_next = (p_xi_0 + mu*(p_xi_1 - p_xi_0), q_xi_0 + mu*(q_xi_1 - q_xi_0)).
gkr-4.soundness_html: The root zero-check is the defining LogUp identity: p_cross = 0 means the numerator sum telescopes to zero, and q_cross = q0_claim ties the GKR recursion to the observed root denominator claim. Linear interpolation with mu is algebraically constrained within the AIR.

default_scene.note: GkrLayerAir dispatches a j-round cubic sumcheck to GkrLayerSumcheckAir via GkrSumcheckInputBus. Each sumcheck round observes (ev1, ev2, ev3) on TranscriptBus, samples a challenge, interpolates at the sampled point, and chains to the next round.

flow.note: Per-layer dispatch: GkrLayerAir sends (layer_idx, claim, tidx) on GkrSumcheckInputBus; GkrLayerSumcheckAir receives and runs the sumcheck; the final (claim_out, eq_at_r_prime) returns on GkrSumcheckOutputBus.

transition.note: Within a layer, round_idx starts at 0, increments by 1 per row, and must reach layer_idx - 1 on the final row. GkrSumcheckChallengeBus threads challenges between rounds.

gkr-5.constraints_html: GkrLayerAir dispatches sumcheck to GkrLayerSumcheckAir via GkrSumcheckInputBus. GkrLayerSumcheckAir has round_idx starting at 0, incrementing by 1, reaching layer_idx - 1 on the last round (forcing exactly j sub-rounds for layer j). Each round observes (ev1, ev2, ev3) on TranscriptBus, samples r_i, performs cubic interpolation at r_i, and incrementally updates eq. The final result is sent back on GkrSumcheckOutputBus.
gkr-5.soundness_html: The sumcheck chain pairing (GkrSumcheckInputBus/OutputBus) forces exactly K-1 sumcheck invocations, one per non-root layer. The triangular polynomial structure (layer j has j sub-rounds, verifier_proof_shape line 404) is enforced by the round_idx boundary constraint. Cubic interpolation and eq update are constrained algebraically; the sampled r values are pinned by the transcript.

default_scene.note: For each non-root layer, GkrLayerAir samples lambda, receives the sumcheck result on GkrSumcheckOutputBus, and enforces expected_claim == (p_cross + lambda * q_cross) * eq_at_r_prime.

consistency.note: Layer consistency: expected_claim (passed from the previous layer via internal state) must equal (p_cross + lambda * q_cross) * eq_at_r_prime. Any tampering with the observed (p_xi, q_xi) values breaks this identity.

transition.note: Layer-to-layer chain: GkrLayerAir row i computes the claim passed to row i+1 via the internal state chain; layer_idx increments by 1 per row.

gkr-6.constraints_html: GkrLayerAir receives the sumcheck final output on GkrSumcheckOutputBus and checks expected_claim == (p_cross + lambda * q_cross) * eq_at_r_prime where expected_claim is the (batched) claim passed in from the previous layer. It then observes (p_xi_0, q_xi_0, p_xi_1, q_xi_1), samples mu_j, and computes the next layer's claim via linear interpolation.
gkr-6.soundness_html: The layer consistency check binds the sumcheck output to the observed layer claims, so any tampering with the per-layer (p_xi, q_xi) values fails. The chain of layer claims is threaded by GkrLayerInputBus/OutputBus (internal permutation), so K layers are forced.

default_scene.note: When n_max > n_logup (or n_logup = 0), GkrXiSamplerAir samples additional xi coordinates. GkrInputAir hands off the starting idx/tidx via GkrXiSamplerBus; the sampler returns control once it has emitted the required number of challenges.

handoff.note: Round-trip on GkrXiSamplerBus: GkrInputAir sends the starting (idx, tidx); GkrXiSamplerAir advances and sends the ending (idx, tidx) back. Imbalanced multiplicity would fail verification.

transition.note: Each row advances idx by 1 and tidx by D_EF = 4. The sampled xi is emitted on XiRandomnessBus at the corresponding idx.

gkr-7.constraints_html: GkrXiSamplerAir samples num_challenges = n_max + l_skip - has_interactions * K additional xi coordinates from TranscriptBus. It receives the starting tidx from GkrInputAir via GkrXiSamplerBus and sends each sampled value on XiRandomnessBus with the appropriate idx.
gkr-7.soundness_html: The number of additional coordinates is deterministic from (n_logup, n_max, l_skip). GkrInputAir's send/receive on GkrXiSamplerBus forces the correct count; when n_logup = 0 all xi coordinates come from this AIR. is_n_max_greater must be 0 when n_max = n_logup > 0, else the sampler leaves GkrXiSamplerBus unbalanced.

default_scene.note: The xi vector is assembled by three senders on the XiRandomnessBus permutation: GkrLayerAir (mu_{K-1} at idx=0), GkrLayerSumcheckAir (last-layer r values at idx=1..K-1), GkrXiSamplerAir (extras at idx=K..). Batch-constraint consumers read by idx.

flow.note: XiRandomnessBus is a permutation bus; its receivers (EqNsAir, EqSharpUniAir) constrain exact indexing. Mismatched entries cause imbalance.

boundary.note: The first xi coordinate is the mu from the last GKR layer (row -1 of GkrLayerAir).

gkr-8.constraints_html: GkrLayerAir sends (idx=0, mu_{K-1}) on XiRandomnessBus. GkrLayerSumcheckAir sends the K-1 sumcheck challenges from the last layer at idx = 1..K-1. GkrXiSamplerAir sends any additional coordinates at idx = K.. Total: n_global + l_skip messages.
gkr-8.soundness_html: XiRandomnessBus is a permutation bus; its receivers (BatchConstraint's EqNsAir, EqSharpUniAir; Stacking's consumers) constrain exact indexing. Mismatched entries cause imbalance. The xi values are all transcript samples, pinned by the sponge (T).

default_scene.note: GkrInputAir receives the last GKR layer's (numer_claim, denom_claim) on GkrLayerOutputBus, assembles gkr_claim = [numer, denom - alpha_logup], and sends it to BatchConstraint via BatchConstraintModuleBus. It also emits tidx_cf on ConstraintsFoldingInputBus.

flow.note: The full GKR→BC handoff: GkrLayerOutputBus carries the internal GKR output back to GkrInputAir, which rewrites the second component (subtract alpha_logup) and forwards via BatchConstraintModuleBus.

folding-schedule.note: GkrInputAir also emits tidx_cf = tidx_bc - D_EF on ConstraintsFoldingInputBus so BC's ConstraintsFoldingAir samples lambda at the right transcript slot.

gkr-9.constraints_html: GkrInputAir receives the last layer's (numer_claim, denom_claim) via internal GkrLayerOutputBus, assembles gkr_claim = [numer_claim, denom_claim - alpha_logup], and sends (tidx_bc, gkr_claim) on BatchConstraintModuleBus. It also sends tidx_cf = tidx_bc - D_EF on ConstraintsFoldingInputBus for BC's lambda sampling.
gkr-9.soundness_html: The output claim is an affine combination of sponge-pinned values (last layer's claims, alpha_logup), so it is determined by W. The handoff tidx values are deterministic functions of tidx_end and the GKR schedule, ensuring the BC module samples at the correct transcript positions.
